fields.description:
# Plan-review Pass-1 finding-memory (recall fix)

## Why
Verdict-flip on identical code is a RECALL problem: in flipping same-material review pairs the decisive
would-block findings had validity ~0.99 and 14/17 flips were because the other run simply MISSED the valid
finding. Improving finder recall closes the gap; tightening thresholds cannot.

## What (independence-preserving: a POST-Pass-1 recall step, NOT a Pass-1 finder injection)
Re-surface a ticket's prior-review findings that the fresh Pass-1 finder MISSED, then let the UNCHANGED
Pass-2 verifier decide whether they still hold — so a genuinely-resolved concern is dropped, not re-blocked.

- LOAD: read the most-recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar via the EXISTING `sidecar.latest_review_result(ticket_id)`
  reader; take prior findings that scored NEAR/ABOVE the bar. Concrete rule: `priority >= 0.5` AND
  `decision in {BLOCK, ADVISORY}` (i.e. exclude DROPPED / low-validity), capped to the top N=12 by priority
  to bound cost.
- RECALL (strictly AFTER Pass-1): compute the fresh findings' `norm_id` set; for each prior concern whose
  `norm_id` is NOT in that set (the fresh finder MISSED it), add it as a RECALL CANDIDATE finding (its prior
  `finding`/`suggested_fix`/`criteria`/`location`, tagged `_recall: true`). Dedup candidates by `norm_id`.
- VERIFY (unchanged): recall candidates flow through the SAME Pass-2 verifier + Pass-3 decision as any
  finding — each judged INDEPENDENTLY against the CURRENT plan. A concern the current plan resolved fails
  validity (< 0.5) and is DROPPED by Pass-2, so a stale recalled finding can NEVER block on memory alone.

## Structural-independence invariant (explicitly preserved — the reason for the post-Pass-1 design)
The codebase enforces finder independence "by construction, not by prompt assertion": the pinned test
`test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` (tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py) asserts the
Pass-1 finder / `verify_instructions` NEVER carry prior findings — prior findings reach ONLY the Pass-2
novelty seam (`verify.prior_findings_block`). ADR 0008 Invariant 1 is the same rule.
This design does NOT inject prior findings into the Pass-1 finder: the finder runs byte-unchanged, so
`test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` and ADR 0008 Invariant 1 stay green. Prior findings enter
ONLY as post-Pass-1 recall CANDIDATES that are verified on their own merits (never merged into the finder's
context; never blocking without independent Pass-2 confirmation). A NEW test pins that the finder
instructions remain prior-free while recall candidates are added strictly after Pass-1.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` — a `prior_concerns(ticket_id, repo_root)` reader that reuses
  `latest_review_result` + `norm_id` (no new fingerprinting algorithm).
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py` (`run_pass1`) — the post-Pass-1 recall step (the fresh finder is
  unchanged; `ctx` already carries `ticket_id` + `repo_root`). Cost is the recalled candidates' Pass-2
  verification tokens, NOT an extra finder call.
- `tests/unit/` — recall + resolved-drop + independence-preserved tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prior-review findings (filtered `priority >= 0.5` AND `decision in {BLOCK, ADVISORY}`, capped to 12)
      that the fresh Pass-1 finder MISSED (by `norm_id`) are re-surfaced as recall candidates AFTER Pass-1;
      they are NOT injected into the Pass-1 finder context and are deduped by `norm_id`.
- [ ] The Pass-1 finder is byte-unchanged: `test_prior_findings_only_reach_the_novelty_seam` still passes,
      and a NEW test asserts `run_pass1`'s finder instructions never contain prior-finding text (independence
      by construction preserved; ADR 0008 Invariant 1 upheld).
- [ ] No additional LLM finder call beyond the existing Pass-1 (recall candidates reuse the existing Pass-2
      verifier; the only added cost is the candidates' verification tokens).
- [ ] Proving command `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_pass1_recall.py -v` (all green), covering: (a) a
      prior finding the fresh run MISSED is re-surfaced as a recall candidate; (b) a prior finding the fresh
      run ALSO found is NOT double-surfaced (norm_id dedup); (c) a recalled candidate whose current-plan
      verification FAILS validity is DROPPED by Pass-2, not re-blocked; (d) with no prior sidecar, recall is a
      no-op. Recall-lift on same-`material_fingerprint` pairs is measured OFFLINE by the calibration child
      (raptorial-galloping-dragon), not gated here.
